Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Health of Animals Act (biosecurity on farms)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Health of Animals Act (biosecurity on farms)

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Agriculture and Agri-Food as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on OctoberÂ 18, 2023

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Health of Animals Act to make it an offence to enter, without lawful authority or excuse, a place in which animals are kept if doing so could reasonably be expected to result in the exposure of the animals to a disease or toxic substance that is capable of affecting or contaminating them.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Health of Animals Act is amended by adding the following after section 9:

Section.MarginalNote: Exposure of animals to disease or toxic substance
Section.Text: No person shall, without lawful authority or excuse, enter a building or other enclosed place in which animals are kept, or take in any animal or thing, if their entering such a place or taking in the animal or thing could reasonably be expected to result in the exposure of the animals to a disease or toxic substance that is capable of affecting or contaminating them.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 65(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Every person who contravenes any provision of this Act, other than section 9.1 or 15, or the regulations or who refuses or neglects to perform any duty imposed by or under the Act or the regulations is guilty of
Section 65 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exposure of animals to disease â individuals
Subsection.Text: Every individual who contravenes section 9.1 is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding twenty-five thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand dollars or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding one year, or to both.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exposure of animals to disease â other persons
Subsection.Text: Every person, other than an individual, that contravenes section 9.1 is guilty of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred thousand dollars; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an indictable offence and liable to a fine not exceeding five hundred thousand dollars.
